title: Guidelines to prevent Arbitrary Use of Powers to Evict Genuine Tenants from Public Premises under the Control of PSUs/Financial Institutions
description: Guidelines to prevent Arbitrary Use of Powers to Evict Genuine Tenants from Public Premises under the Control of PSUs/Financial Institutions

text: GUIDELINES TO PREVENT ARBITRARY USE ffi POWERS TO EVICT GENUINE TENANTS TROH PUBLIC PREMISES UNDER THE CONTRG. @F-.., PUBLIC SECTOR UNDERTAKINGSIFINAITffiTAI, INSTITUTIONS1 i 1. The question.of notification of .quidelines to prevent arbihary rE of powers_to evict genuine tenants from public premises uhder the cond-dftrfufE Sector Undertakings/financial institutions has been under consideratian of fte Government for some time past. 2. To prevent arbitrary use of powers to evict genuine tenants from publfo premise_s a$ to qlt the qqe_9f p-owe.rs by the Estate officers appointed'ur$et sgct1o13 of the PP(E) Act, 1971, it has beeri decided by Govemmii,t to tay donn the following guidelines: (i) The provisions of the Public Premises (Eviction of Unauthorised occupants) Act,197l IP.P. (E) Act, t971l should be used primarily to evict totally unauthorised occupants of the premised of puibti. authorities-or subletees, or emplojrees who have ieased to be li ttreir service and thus ineligible for ociupation of the premises. (ii) The provisions of the P.P.(E) Act, 1977 shourd not be resorted to - either with a commercial motive or to secure vacant possession of the premises in order to accommodate their own employees, where the premises were in occupation of the original tenhnt-s to whom the pr.emise.s were let either by the public authorities or the persons from whom the premises were acquired. (iii) A person in occupation of any premises should not be treated or dedared to be an unauthorised occupant merely on service of notice of termination of tenancy, but me fact of unairthorized occupation shall be decided by following the due procedure of law. Furthirr, the conhacfual agreement shall not be wound up by takins advantaee of the proviqions of the P.P.(E) Act,7971,. At the'same Eme, it wfl be open to- the public authority to secure periodic revision of rent in terms of the provisions of the Rent Conirol Act in each state or to move under genuine grounds under the Rent conhol Act for resuming possession. In other words, the public authorities would have rights similar to private landlords und6r the Rent Control Act in dealing with genuine legal tenants. (iv) It is necessary to give no room for allegations that evictions were selectively resorted to for the purpose oT securing an unwarranted increase in rent, 9r tha! a change in tenancy was permitted in order to benefit particular individuals or institutiohs. Ln drder to avoid such imputations or abuse of dixretionary powers, the release of premises or change of tenancy should be di:cided at the level of Board of Directors of Public Sector Undertakings. (v) All the Public Undertakings should immediately review aU pending cases before the Estate Officer or Courts with reference [o tl:J guidelines, and withdraw eviction proceedings against gErunE tenants on_grounds otherwise than as provided- undei 0rc guidelines. The provisions under the P.P. (E) Act, 1971 should be Ed henceforth only in accordance with these guidelines. 3.
